description: A dynamic router of the first channel (pair)

parameterGroups.label: Channel Selection
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters together define which channels are selected and how many are used.

parameterGroups.label: Output Behaviour
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters control how the selected channels are routed to the output.

gaps.question: SelectOutput has range 0-1 step 1 with a converter. What do the two values mean? Does it toggle between selecting input channels vs output channels?
gaps.context: The converter text labels will clarify this binary toggle.

gaps.question: ClearOtherChannels defaults to 1 (on). When enabled, are non-selected channels zeroed? When disabled, do non-selected channels pass through unchanged?
gaps.context: Determines whether the selector acts as an isolator (clear) or a swapper (no clear).

gaps.question: How does the selector route channels? If ChannelIndex=2 and NumChannels=2, does it copy channels 2-3 to channels 0-1? Or does it shift the entire buffer?
gaps.context: The description says 'router of the first channel (pair)' suggesting it moves selected channels to the front of the buffer.

gaps.question: With IsPolyphonic true, can different voices select different channels via modulated ChannelIndex? How does per-voice channel routing work?
gaps.context: Polyphonic channel selection could enable creative voice-per-channel effects.

notes: routing.selector has multiple images available showing different configurations (1->2, 12->34, 3->1). SelectOutput and ClearOtherChannels are boolean toggles with text converters. The description 'A dynamic router of the first channel (pair)' is somewhat vague. IsPolyphonic suggests per-voice channel selection is possible.
